analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet asserts that human rights are under attack globally and says the EU is acting via the new EU Global Human Rights Sanctions Regime, framed as a powerful tool, with hashtag #EU4HumanRights. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – not explicitly about history or past achievements; not promoted. EU2 (united) – no mention of unity or agreement among member states; not promoted. EU3 (useful for its citizens) – focuses on global human rights action, not internal citizen benefits; not promoted. EU4 (avant-garde political actor) – implies leadership/pioneering by introducing a new sanctions regime as a powerful tool; partially fits the EU as a pioneer, but the emphasis is more on moral leadership than regulatory innovation; weak support. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – strong fit: positions the EU at the forefront of defending human rights worldwide via a sanctions regime. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – by acting globally to protect human rights, it implies contributing to global well-being; moderate support. Final determination: Clearly EU5; also EU6. EU4 is less directly supported, so exclude.
